title: NSRTC 2026
subtitle: A three-day conclave advancing science, technology, and innovation
summary: Scientists, researchers, industry leaders, policymakers, innovators, and students met for interdisciplinary discussions on emerging research and technology.
overview: The programme combined invited talks and parallel technical sessions with round-table discussions, poster presentations, project exhibitions, and a Scholars-to-Scientists Connect initiative.
The conference brought together contributors from universities, national laboratories, research institutes, industry, and public organisations.
themes: Reimagining the future with artificial intelligence

programme.label: Day 1
programme.text: Opening sessions considered major scientific and engineering prizes, followed by the inaugural programme, research and entrepreneurship awards, keynote addresses, and a lecture on national wealth through science and technology.

programme.label: Days 2–3
programme.text: Four parallel research tracks covered artificial intelligence, sustainable energy, advanced materials, semiconductors, health technologies, biotechnology, quantum technologies, and indigenous innovation.

title: NSRTC 2025
subtitle: Science, innovation, and national research priorities
summary: A national meeting of scientists, technologists, researchers, policy leaders, and students organised around research priorities for India.
overview: The programme opened with plenary sessions on Nobel Prize-recognised work in medicine, chemistry, physics, and engineering, followed by specialist talks and a formal inaugural session.
Technical sessions and round tables connected fundamental research, applied science, technology development, and questions of national capacity.

programme.label: Special talks
programme.text: Ashutosh Sharma spoke on science and technology for national development; Arindam Ghosh on materials for quantum technologies; and Ajit Kembhavi on the life and scientific journey of Jayant Vishnu Narlikar.

programme.label: Scientific programme
programme.text: Parallel sessions covered artificial intelligence, materials and devices, energy and sustainability, healthcare, pharmaceuticals, and biotechnology, with posters and round-table discussions.
